what_it_does: Audits each described property of the SEED geometric substrate against the live instrument surface, marks visibility status with emojis, and sequences remaining gaps as beats.
observations: It is a living doc updated in place.
Uses a status key: visible, present but not legible, missing, data/keystone-blocked.
Lists numbered gaps plus a historical entry, already-done items, carry-over polish, and re-critic verdicts.
Heavy use of commit hashes, dates, names, engine fields, API endpoints, and embedding layer names.
Gap #1 says the circle/square coincidence on the axes is geometrically correct but not marked visually.
Gap #2 tracks rendering misfit outside the box and the corner as a grow-the-box signal.
Gap #7 reports the pplx nucleation layer is live via API but the front-end layer picker is pending.
Design-critic PASS/FAIL verdicts are recorded as first-class status.
standouts: The central seed claim — circle and square agree only on the axes — is latent in the engine but not marked on the surface.
Backend nucleation is verified live through the pplx embedding layer, producing real born candidate operators and residue.
The lens menu is reframed as axis-assignment, but that framing is not yet legible to the operator.
Design-critic verdicts are tracked alongside code commits.
conventions: Status emoji key at the top.
Sequenced gap list with unblocked and central items first.
Cross-references to other files, API endpoints, and seed section numbers (§1, §2, etc.).
concerns: The file mixes audit, build log, design critique, and API verification in one document.
Some gaps overlap, for example #2 and #6 both discuss the corner reading.
Status labels are informal and not machine-readable.
References to external sessions and people may decay without context.
Living doc updated in place risks losing version history if not tracked.
notes: A design-to-code audit trail that maps abstract geometric claims from THE SEED to concrete instrument gaps, commits, and live API verification.
questions: What exactly is the SEED equation and where is it fully specified?
What is the relationship between the 12 lens FORMS and the seed sections?
Which gaps have owners or target dates?
What does green-paint risk mean precisely here?
How will the SURFACE zone layer picker coordinate with this audit?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to audit described properties versus the live surface; the file does that while also acting as a build log, design-critic transcript, and backend verification record, slightly exceeding a pure audit.
apparent_themes: Design critique as a quality gate
intention_signals: The file is trying to keep the instrument honest to its underlying geometric and semantic model by making every abstract property visible and operable, and to coordinate design, backend, and surface work through a shared gap list.
novelty: It treats a geometric equation as a live UI audit checklist, using status emojis, design-critic verdicts, and commit hashes as first-class audit evidence.
contribution: It preserves the mapping between the project's foundational geometric narrative and the concrete instrument features, gaps, and verification results, which a fused system would need when tracing why a UI choice exists.
summary_for_embedding: EQUATION-AUDIT.md is a living design-to-code audit that walks the SEED geometric substrate section by section, checks whether each described property is visible on the live instrument, and sequences remaining gaps with status emojis, commit references, and design-critic verdicts.
intention_for_embedding: To keep the instrument surface accountable to the SEED equation by tracking which abstract geometric properties are built, visible, blocked, or still missing.
